benefits: GI Bill (1944) transformed America: 7.8M used education benefits, created the middle class, built the suburbs. VA home loans backed $1.6T in mortgages.
ptsd: Called "combat fatigue" or "shell shock." Estimated 30-50% experienced symptoms. No formal treatment programs.

benefits: Korean War GI Bill less generous than WWII version.
ptsd: Called "The Forgotten War" — veterans received far less recognition than WWII or Vietnam vets. Higher rates of substance abuse and homelessness.

benefits: Agent Orange Act (1991) finally acknowledged exposure. VA spent $80B+ on Agent Orange-related care.
ptsd: 30% of Vietnam veterans experienced PTSD (830,000 people). PTSD was first formally defined in 1980 partly due to Vietnam veterans' advocacy. 9,000 Vietnam veterans died by suicide in the first 5 years after returning.

benefits: Gulf War Illness affected 250,000+ veterans (34%). Symptoms: fatigue, pain, cognitive issues. VA acknowledged in 2010.
ptsd: 12% of Gulf War veterans experienced PTSD. Gulf War Illness (GWI) remains controversial — causes include oil well fires, depleted uranium, nerve gas exposure.

benefits: Post-9/11 GI Bill (2008): $78B in education benefits disbursed. PACT Act (2022) expanded VA healthcare for toxic exposure.
ptsd: 20% of Afghanistan/Iraq veterans diagnosed with PTSD or depression. 30,177 active duty and veteran suicides since 9/11 — 4x the number killed in combat. 17 veterans die by suicide per day.

benefits: Same as Afghanistan — Post-9/11 GI Bill. PACT Act covers burn pit and toxic exposure.
ptsd: Similar rates to Afghanistan. TBI (traumatic brain injury) affected 380,000+ from IED blasts. Called the "signature injury" of Iraq.
